---
title: "R Output Review"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
---

```{r setup}
input_dir <- dirname(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE))
repo_root <- normalizePath(file.path(input_dir, "..", ".."))
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = repo_root)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)

library(knitr)
```

This file collects the CSV outputs written by
`code/r/replicate_descriptives.Rmd` into one human-readable HTML review page.

```{r helpers}
table_dir <- file.path(repo_root, "results", "tables", "r")

table_titles <- c(
  "replication_status.csv" = "Replication Status",
  "sample_sizes.csv" = "Sample Sizes",
  "published_target_checks.csv" = "Published Target Checks",
  "table1_descriptives.csv" = "Table 1 Descriptive Statistics",
  "table1_differences.csv" = "Table 1 Mean Differences",
  "table1_bootstrap_ci.csv" = "Table 1 Bootstrap Confidence Intervals",
  "nonparametric_tests.csv" = "Nonparametric Tests",
  "table2_response_categories.csv" = "Table 2 Response Categories",
  "table2_fisher_tests.csv" = "Table 2 Fisher Tests",
  "table3_gcos_means.csv" = "Table 3 GCOS Means",
  "table3_gcos_tests.csv" = "Table 3 GCOS Tests",
  "table4_regression_check.csv" = "Table 4 Regression Check",
  "result6_control_choice_proportions.csv" = "Result 6 Control Choice Proportions",
  "result6_tests.csv" = "Result 6 Tests",
  "result6_bootstrap_ci.csv" = "Result 6 Bootstrap Confidence Interval",
  "appendix_a1_partial_checks.csv" = "Appendix A1 Partial Checks",
  "appendix_a1_demographics_audit.csv" = "Appendix A1 Demographics Audit"
)

csv_files <- file.path(table_dir, names(table_titles))
missing_files <- csv_files[!file.exists(csv_files)]
if (length(missing_files) > 0) {
  stop("Missing expected CSV outputs: ", paste(basename(missing_files), collapse = ", "))
}
```

```{r tables, results='asis'}
for (file in csv_files) {
  title <- table_titles[[basename(file)]]
  data <- read.csv(file, check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  cat("\n\n## ", title, "\n\n", sep = "")
  print(kable(data))
}
```
